name: One-Pass (track min price)
summary: Walk the days once, remembering the cheapest price seen so far; at each day check the profit of selling today against that minimum, and keep the best.
visualizationIntent: init: prices shown as a row of day-cells; min_price starts as prices[0]. loop: pointer i advances to today (current cell) and we read prices[i]. check: compare today's price to the cheapest-so-far — if lower, today becomes the new cheapest buy day (special). update: compute profit = today - cheapest and, if it beats the best, max_profit jumps (key event). The cheapest-so-far cell to the left stays special (the buy point); a readout shows 'sell - buy = profit · best'. return: the overall cheapest day stays special and the result panel holds the max profit.

lineExplanations.3: Remember the first day's price as the cheapest buy price seen so far.
lineExplanations.4: Start the best profit at 0 — if prices only fall, doing nothing (profit 0) is the answer.
lineExplanations.5: Scan every day in order; each is a potential day to sell on.
lineExplanations.6: Read today's price into a variable so the profit check reads clearly.
lineExplanations.7: Check whether today's price is lower than the cheapest price seen so far.
lineExplanations.8: It is — record today as the new cheapest possible buy price.
lineExplanations.9: Compute the profit of buying at the cheapest price so far and selling today.
lineExplanations.10: Check whether selling today would beat the best profit found so far.
lineExplanations.11: It does — lock in this profit as the new best.
lineExplanations.12: Return the largest profit found across the single pass (0 if none was possible).

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — the class wrapper the platform expects your method to live inside.
syntaxExplanations.2: def maxProfit(self, prices): — defines the method; prices is the list of daily stock prices.
syntaxExplanations.3: min_price = prices[0] — initialise the cheapest-seen price to the first day's price.
syntaxExplanations.4: max_profit = 0 — the best profit so far; 0 represents making no trade.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(len(prices)): — loop i over every day index 0 .. n-1.
syntaxExplanations.6: price = prices[i] — grab today's price into a local for readability.
syntaxExplanations.7: if price < min_price: — is today cheaper than every earlier day?
syntaxExplanations.8: min_price = price — update the cheapest buy price to today's.
syntaxExplanations.9: profit = price - min_price — profit of selling today after buying at the cheapest.
syntaxExplanations.10: if profit > max_profit: — did selling today beat our best profit?
syntaxExplanations.11: max_profit = profit — record the new best profit.
syntaxExplanations.12: return max_profit — hand back the largest profit (0 if none).
